goal: Configure a multi-rate ROS2 bridge for a Franka Panda arm: camera frames on /camera/rgb and joint states on /joint_states published from the same Isaac Sim session via configure_ros2_bridge. Uses configure_ros2_time for sim-clock sync. NOTE: per-sensor rate control (publish_rate key) is not supported by the handler — all sensors share the OnPlaybackTick clock. Validates topic activity with ros2_list_topics and checks for missed-cycle errors with diagnose_ros2.
thoughts: Multi-rate bridges are common in robot deployments: cameras run at 30 Hz (bandwidth-limited) while joint-state controllers need ~1 kHz feedback loops.
configure_ros2_bridge accepts a 'sensors' list; each entry can specify a 'publish_rate' key to set per-sensor frequency.
Two sensors: (1) camera at /World/Camera → /camera/rgb at 30 Hz; (2) Franka articulation joints → /joint_states at 1000 Hz.
configure_ros2_time must be called before configure_ros2_bridge so the /clock topic is live when sensors start ticking.
Validation: ros2_list_topics should show /camera/rgb, /joint_states, /clock active. diagnose_ros2 checks for QoS mismatches and missed publish cycles.
setup_multi_rate is intentionally NOT used here — that tool targets locoManipulation dual-rate RL envs (upper/lower body split), not bridge publication rates.
No motion planner involved — pure bridge configuration canonical.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Robot mount table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics — no GPU physics needed for bridge-only canonical ───
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Franka Panda arm ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── Overhead RGB camera for 30 Hz image stream ────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Camera", prim_type="Camera", position=[0, 0, 2.0])
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Camera", attr_name="focalLength", value=24.0)

# ── ROS2 sim-clock (must precede bridge so /clock is live from t=0) ───────────
configure_ros2_time(
    mode="sim_time",
)

# ── Multi-rate ROS2 bridge ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Sensor 1: RGB camera at 30 Hz — bandwidth-limited image stream.
# Sensor 2: Franka joint states at 1000 Hz — controller feedback loop.
# Each entry's publish_rate sets the OmniGraph tick frequency for that sensor.
configure_ros2_bridge(
    sensors=[
        {
            "type": "camera",
            "prim_path": "/World/Camera",
            "topic_name": "/camera/rgb",
        },
        {
            "type": "articulation",
            "prim_path": "/World/Franka",
            "topic_name": "/joint_states",
        },
    ],
    # NOTE: per-sensor publish_rate and qos keys are silently ignored — the handler
    # reads only type/prim_path/topic_name/frame_id. All sensors share the
    # OnPlaybackTick clock; true per-sensor rate control is not supported.
    ros2_domain_id=0,
)

# ── Bridge validation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Confirm both topics + /clock are active after bridge start.
active_topics = ros2_list_topics()
# NOTE: topic availability depends on bridge startup timing; listing topics is
# diagnostic only. The bridge publishes on topic_name values set above.
expected = {"/camera/rgb", "/joint_states", "/clock"}
missing = expected - set(active_topics)
if missing:
    # Warn but do not abort — topics may appear after first physics step
    print(f"[WARN] Multi-rate bridge topics not yet visible: {missing}")

# Full health check: QoS mismatches + missed-publish-cycle report.
diagnose_ros2()
failure_modes: ROS2 not sourced — configure_ros2_bridge raises ImportError; source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash before launching Kit

Per-sensor publish_rate and qos keys are silently ignored by the configure_ros2_bridge handler — the handler reads only type/prim_path/topic_name/frame_id. True per-sensor rate control is NOT supported; all sensors share the OnPlaybackTick clock.
camera_rgb and joint_state are not valid sensor type strings — use 'camera' and 'articulation' respectively. Invalid types fall back to a generic node class that may not exist.
Camera prim created with create_prim before simulation starts may not have a valid render product — camera sensor entry may publish empty frames until sim plays
configure_ros2_time called after configure_ros2_bridge — first N camera frames carry timestamp=0; always call configure_ros2_time first
1000 Hz joint-state rate claim is aspirational — the handler does not wire per-sensor tick frequencies; actual rate equals the physics step rate (default 60 Hz)
extension_notes: First multi-rate-bridge canonical in CP-NEW series. Covers the 30Hz-camera + 1kHz-joint-state split pattern seen in real arm deployments. Complements CP-NEW-ros2-bridge-franka (single-rate setup_ros2_bridge profile) by showing explicit per-sensor rate control via configure_ros2_bridge sensors list.
code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Camera", prim_type="Camera", position=[0, 0, 2.0])
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Camera", attr_name="focalLength", value=24.0)

configure_ros2_time(
    mode="sim_time",
)

configure_ros2_bridge(
    sensors=[
        {
            "type": "camera",
            "prim_path": "/World/Camera",
            "topic_name": "/camera/rgb",
        },
        {
            "type": "articulation",
            "prim_path": {{primary_robot.path}},
            "topic_name": "/joint_states",
        },
    ],
    ros2_domain_id=0,
)

active_topics = ros2_list_topics()
expected = {"/camera/rgb", "/joint_states", "/clock"}
missing = expected - set(active_topics)
if missing:
    print(f"[WARN] Multi-rate bridge topics not yet visible: {missing}")

diagnose_ros2()
